sources: Avoma call 2025-09-09 (CLM Demo Test run with Jordan)
Avoma call 2025-10-13 (Zycus X ARUP - CLM Demo)
metrics.narrative: ARUP's pain is well documented as manual, error-prone contract handling. Champion Michael Tutton said the current authoring/redlining process is 'entirely manual' and that reviewing a 10-page third-party agreement requires him to 'manually go and find what's missing' (CLM Demo Test run with Jordan, 9 Sep 2025). Zycus showed community-average cycle time (17 days) against ARUP's own estimated ~20-day average during the CLM demo on 13 Oct 2025, but ARUP itself has not produced a quantified savings or ROI figure, and no dollar-value business case is on record as of the 29 Jun 2026 RFP release.

champion.narrative: Michael Tutton has been Zycus's champion at ARUP for 5-plus years ('total total champion,' 'been in Horizon multiple times,' CLM Demo Test run with Jordan, 9 Sep 2025) and personally drove leadership to approve a formal RFP naming Zycus (Next Step entries, 1 Jun 2026 and 29 Jun 2026). He has repeatedly acknowledged the internal sell is difficult ('it's still gonna be a hard sell,' Call with Michael Tutton, 4 Feb 2026) and the deal has stalled for weeks at a time awaiting stakeholder availability (Call with Michael Tutton, 12 Nov 2025) — a strong but effectively solo-carrying champion facing real internal resistance.

competition.narrative: Conga (bundled with Salesforce) is the entrenched incumbent; ARUP explicitly chose in September 2025 to invest in Zycus rather than fund further Conga/Salesforce enhancements (ARUP Discussion with Zycus, 8 Sep 2025), and by the 16 Oct 2025 debrief Conga was described as 'primarily a document repository with basic signature and redlining capabilities.' Coupa was named once as an inferior alternative for CLM (Zycus CLM Demo Debrief, 3 Sep 2025) and has not resurfaced since. The formal RFP released 29 Jun 2026 opens the field to unnamed co-bidders whose identity is not yet known to Zycus.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is deeply and repeatedly articulated directly by the champion: manual authoring and redlining in Word and Salesforce/Conga cause version-control issues and lost time (CLM Demo Test run with Jordan, 9 Sep 2025); the earlier CLM demo debrief noted 'significant confusion' from unclear redlining and version control (Zycus CLM Demo Debrief, 3 Sep 2025); and ARUP's own account of the first CLM demo ('it was a failure... it really was') underscores how central resolving this pain is to the deal (ARUP Discussion with Zycus, 8 Sep 2025).

sources: Absence across all read sources through 2026-07-09
paper_process.narrative: No contracting artifacts (MSA, SOW, Order Form) have surfaced in any call, task, or next-step entry to date. The deal remains pre-proposal-submission inside a formal RFP; paper process has not begun.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is confirmed in Salesforce. Multiple calls reference a senior, director-level stakeholder who ultimately controls the Conga-vs-Zycus decision — he requested a full Salesforce/Conga-vs-Zycus gap analysis (Catch up with Zycus, 9 Jan 2026) and was described as 'very skeptical' even after the October 2025 demos (Zycus Debrief, 16 Oct 2025). This person cannot be matched to a named Salesforce contact, has declined at least one invite to engage directly (ARUP Discussion with Zycus, 8 Sep 2025, re: a different named contact declining), and has never had direct Zycus engagement — all access runs through champion Michael Tutton.

decision_process.narrative: The process has moved from informal internal evaluation to a structured RFP: intent-to-respond, questions, and a proposal deadline logged for 10 Jul 2026, with an IT security questionnaire explicitly reserved for shortlisted vendors after proposals are submitted. Executives have engaged indirectly — reviewing Zycus's Gartner Magic Quadrant news and confirming attendance at a Procurement Pinnacle roundtable on 26 Feb 2026 (Call with Michael Tutton, 4 Feb 2026) — but the path from proposal submission to a final decision is not mapped against the 31 Jul 2026 close date.

decision_criteria.narrative: ARUP built its evaluation criteria through an internal gap analysis of current Salesforce/Conga capabilities vs. Zycus, running at least three rounds of internal meetings with the Conga team by January 2026 (Catch up with Zycus, 9 Jan 2026). The formal RFP released 29 Jun 2026 converts this into scored requirements spanning CLM functionality and granular per-user/per-envelope pricing across iContract, Certinal, and supplier-risk modules, but the specific scoring rubric buyers will apply is not yet visible to Zycus.

engagement.top_event: [Clari - Email Sent] Re: Zycus: RFP – Consolidated Responses to Submitted Questions

items.name: ARUP sends consolidated RFP Q&A to all suppliers
items.summary: Mzia Sturua (ARUP IT PMO) distributed a consolidated document of all questions and answers submitted by RFP participants, formally incorporating them into the RFP documentation and inviting further questions via reply.

items.name: Steve acknowledges receipt
items.summary: Steve Ovadje replied with a brief thank-you confirming receipt of the consolidated Q&A doc — no further questions raised, closing the loop on this exchange for now.

day_summary.overall: ARUP's PMO sent all RFP participants a consolidated Q&A document to keep the competitive process fair and transparent, formally rolling those clarifications into the RFP documentation. Steve simply acknowledged receipt, so the ball is now in ARUP's court to run the rest of the RFP timeline — no open asks on our side beyond monitoring for further updates.

cro_panel.intro: ARUP's champion has pushed this deal through a roughly 14-month internal build, culminating in a formal CLM RFP naming Zycus (released 29 Jun 2026, proposal due 10 Jul 2026; tomorrow).; The read is genuinely mixed; a long-tenured, vocal champion and an explicit internal decision to favor Zycus over enhancing the Conga incumbent, set against an unmapped and reportedly skeptical senior stakeholder, an unresolved commercial package due imminently, and an unknown field of RFP co-bidders

contributions.detail: Close date has been pushed repeatedly (~12 times since the original Dec 2025 target, most recently 31 days) — credibility is low, especially with the post-proposal IT security review not yet started.
No confirmed Economic Buyer; the unmapped senior stakeholder is reportedly skeptical of unseating the Conga/Salesforce incumbent.
RFP release opens the field to unnamed co-bidders alongside the entrenched Conga incumbent — an unmapped competitive threat.
155 days of zero live buyer meetings, with none scheduled, despite an imminent 7/10 proposal deadline.
Partial mitigation: a genuine, long-tenured champion single-handedly pushed the RFP through internal approval, showing the account is not fully dark.

Zero buyer meetings in 90/60/30 days — last live session was Feb 4, 2026 (155 days ago) — and no future meeting is scheduled. ► Book a call with ARUP before the 7/10 proposal deadline.

Close date has been pushed repeatedly, most recently 31 days (6/30→7/31), part of a pattern the packet flags as ~12 pushes since the original Dec 2025 target. ► Stress-test 7/31 against the RFP's own post-proposal IT security review, which hasn't started.
Forecast category is still 'Upside Key Deal,' not Commit, despite the RFP going live — no conviction upgrade recorded.
Next-Step history shows genuine trajectory: stalled management approval → convinced management → formal RFP naming Zycus, all buyer-side movement.

deal_movement.summary: After a stall from Nov 2025 through Jan 2026, internal evaluation activity resumed in Feb 2026 and accelerated through mid-2026, culminating in the formal RFP release on 29 Jun 2026.

forecast_read.math: Today 2026-07-09; close 2026-07-31 = 22 days away. RFP proposal due 2026-07-10 (tomorrow); post-proposal steps (evaluation, reserved IT security questionnaire for shortlisted vendors, executive decision) have no dated milestones and have not begun. Close date has already moved at least 12 times from an original 2025-12-31 target to the current 2026-07-31, per prior sweep record.
forecast_read.reason: The RFP ARUP itself issued has a proposal deadline of 10 Jul 2026 (tomorrow) and explicitly reserves an IT security questionnaire for shortlisted vendors AFTER proposals are submitted, which has not started. A 31 Jul 2026 close leaves ~21 days for evaluation, a security review that hasn't begun, and an executive decision from an unmapped, reportedly skeptical stakeholder — not credible against ARUP's own defined process.
forecast_read.recommended_forecast: Pipeline / Best Case (not Upside Key Deal as currently forecast without evidence to support a 31 Jul close)

critical_signals.text: ARUP's own RFP proposal deadline lands tomorrow (10 Jul 2026) and the required per-user/per-envelope pricing across the contract, e-signature, and supplier-risk modules is still not finalized.

The formal RFP released 29 Jun 2026 opens the field to unnamed competing bidders whose identity Zycus does not yet know, even though ARUP already chose Zycus over expanding its Conga incumbent last year.

The champion's year-long push finally got management to launch a formal RFP naming Zycus, but the last verified two-way conversation on record is a buyer email reply from 24 Jun 2026, with no recorded call since 4 Feb 2026.
ARUP's RFP reserves an IT security and systems review specifically for whichever vendors get shortlisted after proposals are in, and that review has not started.

champion_strength.summary: Michael Tutton is a genuine, long-tenured champion who personally converted management skepticism into an approved formal RFP, but he is carrying the deal largely alone against an unmapped, skeptical senior stakeholder, and the cycle has repeatedly stalled for weeks awaiting his bandwidth.

expansion_context.note: No evidence of a prior Closed-Won ARUP opportunity. The relationship is long-standing (a 2009-era Zycus quote was referenced during a 6 Oct 2025 call as historical context), but no signed contract has ever been confirmed.

items.expected_effect: Stays in the competitive RFP process and avoids elimination on a technicality.

confidence_signals.summary: Deep, verified engagement history from May 2025 through Feb 2026 (13 calls, several deep transcripts) gives strong confidence in the deal's origin story and champion strength, but the most recent five months (Feb-Jun 2026), including the RFP release itself, are known only through Next Step log text with no corroborating call evidence.

north_star_verdict.math: Opportunity created 2025-07-25 (~347 days open as of 2026-07-07). Close date 2026-07-31 is only 24 days away, but the RFP proposal Zycus must submit isn't due until 2026-07-10 (~3 days from today), and the RFP itself reserves a further IT security questionnaire and evaluation step for shortlisted vendors AFTER proposals are in. None of that fits inside the remaining 21 days after submission, so the close date will slip regardless of how the RFP result lands.
evidence: SF Next_Step__c updated 2026-06-29: RFP released with Intent to Respond 6/9, Questions 6/16, Proposal due 7/10/2026
SF CloseDate 2026-07-31 vs proposal deadline 2026-07-10 leaves no time for post-proposal evaluation
north_star_verdict.headline: Michael Tutton's year-long internal campaign finally landed a formal RFP with Zycus guaranteed a seat, but the 31 Jul 2026 close date cannot survive a process whose own proposal deadline (10 Jul 2026) hasn't been met yet.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline (push close date to Q4 2026 at the earliest, pending RFP scoring, security review, and contracting)

flags: With 'several participants' confirmed in ARUP's live RFP (24 Jun 2026), differentiate on AI-assisted simultaneous review and redlining (Merlin) rather than price, since Conga's core documented gap is manual, non-simultaneous review.

Equip champion Michael Tutton with a quantified ROI/savings case before the 10 Jul 2026 proposal, since he has already flagged expecting 'a hard sell' internally and currently has only qualitative pain points to argue with.

No economic buyer or final decision-maker is confirmed in Salesforce this late in the cycle; use the RFP's own promised post-proposal 'demonstrations, reference checks' phase to get in front of the real approver and name them in Salesforce.

ARUP's own Conga/Salesforce team is pitching an in-house enhancement path in parallel to the RFP; the proposal must quantify what Conga structurally cannot do (redlining, risk scoring, D365-integrated obligation reporting), not rely on live demos alone.
No Salesforce-verified economic buyer sits above the sourcing team; push for a review with the director evaluating build-vs-buy before the proposal lands so the decision isn't made on price alone.

Reinforce Zycus's Gartner Magic Quadrant placement (shared with Michael Tutton in Jan 2026) directly inside the RFP response, since it was explicitly flagged as a credibility lever for Michael's internal sell.

Address the unverified director-level stakeholder's specific objections directly (via a tailored ROI case or reference call) rather than routing everything through the champion, given his documented skepticism since Sep 2025.
Book a post-submission debrief/evaluation checkpoint immediately after the 10 Jul 2026 proposal deadline; none is currently on the calendar.
best_practice_check.summary: The deal needs the skeptical senior stakeholder engaged directly before RFP scoring locks in, multi-threading beyond the sole champion, and a scheduled evaluation/debrief touchpoint immediately after tomorrow's proposal submission.

customer_preference.evidence: ARUP's team explicitly chose to invest in Zycus over enhancing Conga ('the discussion confirmed a focus on the Zycus tool to avoid additional costs associated with enhancing the existing Conga tool,' ARUP Discussion with Zycus, 8 Sep 2025) and the champion later convinced management to launch a formal RFP naming Zycus (Next Step, 1 Jun 2026). However, this preference is champion-voiced, not committee-confirmed, and the senior stakeholder above the champion remains skeptical.

competitive_position.summary: Conga (bundled with Salesforce) is the entrenched incumbent that ARUP explicitly chose not to further invest in back in September 2025. Coupa surfaced once as an inferior CLM alternative and has not resurfaced. The formal RFP released 29 Jun 2026 now opens the field to an unknown set of co-bidders, which is the freshest and least-understood competitive risk on the deal.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: ARUP's champion has pushed this deal through a roughly 14-month internal build, culminating in a formal CLM RFP naming Zycus (released 29 Jun 2026, proposal due 10 Jul 2026 - tomorrow). The read is genuinely mixed: a long-tenured, vocal champion and an explicit internal decision to favor Zycus over enhancing the Conga incumbent, set against an unmapped and reportedly skeptical senior stakeholder, an unresolved commercial package due imminently, and an unknown field of RFP co-bidders.

deal_risk.text: Close date (31 Jul 2026) leaves only ~21 days after the 10 Jul 2026 proposal deadline for evaluation, a reserved IT security questionnaire that hasn't started, and a decision — not credible against ARUP's own defined process.

ARUP's own Salesforce/Conga team ran a parallel in-house enhancement gap-analysis case (three internal rounds through Jan 2026), creating a build-vs-buy political risk even if Zycus wins the technical evaluation.

win_position.text: Champion Michael Tutton has driven the deal for years and convinced ARUP management to formally launch the RFP naming Zycus, per the Next Step log through 1 Jun 2026.

ARUP explicitly chose to invest in Zycus rather than fund Conga enhancements: 'the discussion confirmed a focus on the Zycus tool to avoid additional costs associated with enhancing the existing Conga tool' (ARUP Discussion with Zycus, 8 Sep 2025).
No economic buyer is confirmed in Salesforce; the senior stakeholder steering the Conga-vs-Zycus decision remains unverified and was described as skeptical in calls on 8 Sep 2025 and 16 Oct 2025.
The RFP proposal, including finalized per-user/per-envelope pricing across iContract, Certinal, and supplier-risk modules, is due 10 Jul 2026 (tomorrow) and is not yet complete.

deal_momentum.text: ARUP released its formal CLM RFP on 29 Jun 2026 naming Zycus as an included vendor, and a genuine two-way buyer email exchange occurred as recently as 24 Jun 2026.
No Avoma-recorded call has occurred since the 4 Feb 2026 check-in; the last five months of progress are visible only through Next Step log entries, not verified conversations.

customer_commitment.text: Michael Tutton has been a self-described long-tenured champion (referenced as being with Zycus '5, 6, 7 years' in the 9 Sep 2025 call) and personally lobbied leadership to approve the RFP.

The champion has twice acknowledged the internal sell is 'a hard sell' (Call with Michael Tutton, 4 Feb 2026) and that the deal has stalled repeatedly awaiting stakeholder scheduling (Call with Michael Tutton, 12 Nov 2025).

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Zycus is well aligned on core CLM depth (redlining, risk scoring, WordConnect, D365/Salesforce integration) after recovering from an early demo miss, but commercial pricing granularity and the reserved IT security review remain open and exposed ahead of the imminent proposal deadline.

hard.ais_why:
• On the 92-minute 9/2/2025 workshop, the only AI-tagged commitment is Aakriti Srivastava sending Michael Tutton's team a screenshot of the AI capability for identifying risky clauses in contracts. That is a buyer-pulled AI evaluation, but it sits as one item inside a 12-item action list dominated by supplier onboarding, contract approval flow, SAP adapter integration, FedRAMP, and sub-tier mapping.
• Michael Tutton's own action items are organisational, not technical: lead internal discussions on ARUP's current ERP system capabilities, identify CLM gaps, coordinate with other ARUP stakeholders, and evaluate the need for further demos. He is sizing the CLM scope across ARUP rather than pulling on AI. No second AI-specific buyer ask surfaced across the action set or filtered transcript keyword review.
• AI is flagged Important at the account level and Merlin and Merlin Intake are in product scope. The risky-clause ask plus active engagement keeps this above AI Resistant, but no POC, no AI-tied budget, no named AI champion, and a buyer agenda still focused on CLM gap analysis means it stays at the lower end of AI Curious.
